title: Phase 9: service identity migration verification
description: Add migration fixtures and full provider/config/ui regression coverage for Service identity.
acceptance_criteria: Old profiles load; new profiles save Service; go test ./internal/config ./internal/llm ./internal/ui and go test ./... pass.

title: Phase 9: update built-in profiles with service identity
description: Add Service values to built-in profiles such as openai, anthropic, ollama, openrouter, vercel, zai-openai, zai-anthropic.
acceptance_criteria: Config tests prove built-ins set Provider as wire format and Service as upstream identity.

title: Phase 9: route providers by service identity
description: Replace host-based routing in internal/llm/providers.go with Service-aware routing while keeping baseURL overrides valid.
acceptance_criteria: Provider routing tests cover every built-in profile and custom openai-compatible profiles without URL sniffing.

title: Phase 9: design Config/Profile service identity migration
description: Define Service field semantics, JSON profile migration, defaults, and compatibility with Provider as wire format.
acceptance_criteria: Design covers saved profile migration, env overrides, built-ins, and rollback behavior.

title: Phase 8: cancellation race and subprocess tests
description: Add race tests for cancelling bash/read-like work, closing channels, and repeated Esc input.
acceptance_criteria: go test -race ./... passes; tests cover no goroutine/channel deadlock on cancel.

title: Phase 9 epic: service identity field
description: Add explicit service identity to Config/Profile so provider behavior no longer relies on URL sniffing.
acceptance_criteria: Built-in profiles, saved profiles, routing, and provider-specific gates use Service/Identity instead of host guesses.

title: Phase 8: feed user-cancelled result back into fantasy
description: When a tool is cancelled, return a normal fantasy tool result such as user cancelled so the model can continue.
acceptance_criteria: Agent loop continues after Esc x1; transcript includes cancelled tool result; no stream-abort Go error is returned.

title: Phase 8: design per-tool cancellation state machine
description: Map UI key events, active tool IDs, context cancellation, and the user-cancelled tool result contract.
acceptance_criteria: State machine specifies Esc x1/x2/x3 behavior, ownership of cancellation funcs, and transcript effects.

title: Phase 6: add MCP permission and audit boundaries
description: Add explicit controls for enabling MCP servers, logging tool calls, and handling untrusted outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Security checklist passes; dangerous defaults are avoided; docs warn about tool side effects.

title: Phase 6: choose MCP transport/config/security contract
description: Define supported MCP transports, config format, trust model, tool naming, resource exposure, and failure behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Contract covers stdio/http if supported, secrets handling, per-workspace config, and disabled-by-default behavior.

title: Phase 4: PrepareStep regression and cancellation suite
description: Add tests proving PrepareStep callbacks do not deadlock, preserve system prompt behavior, and respect context cancellation.
acceptance_criteria: Focused llm/agent tests and go test -race ./... pass with PrepareStep enabled.

title: Phase 4: define PrepareStep responsibilities and ordering
description: Specify ownership for prompt-cache markers, queued-prompt drain, tool refresh, cancellation checks, and provider gates.
acceptance_criteria: Design notes define ordering and write scopes for each PrepareStep concern.

title: Phase 3: add session JSONL compatibility reader/writer
description: Teach session load/save to understand old llm.Message fields and new fantasy message parts. Write scope: internal/session.
acceptance_criteria: Old fixtures load unchanged; new sessions include fantasy parts; mixed sessions round-trip without data loss.

title: Phase 3: write fantasy-native message/storage contract
description: Define canonical in-memory message type, persisted JSONL shape, compatibility rules, and migration rollback strategy.
acceptance_criteria: Contract names every affected package and includes old-session compatibility requirements before code changes start.

title: Phase 2: verify typed tool migration end-to-end
description: Run focused unit tests, race tests, and a headless tool-call smoke where credentials are available. Update docs if the registration boundary changes.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./internal/llm ./internal/tools ./internal/agent, go test -race ./..., and at least one provider or fake-provider tool-loop smoke are recorded.

title: Phase 2: audit current tool contracts and schema ownership
description: Inventory read/write/edit/search_memory/bash params, outputs, error semantics, and current tests. Write scope: docs or test notes only unless adding tests.
acceptance_criteria: Each tool has documented params, output contract, error behavior, and test gaps; no production behavior changes.

title: Phase 2: add typed tool adapter test harness
description: Create focused tests for typed fantasy tool wrappers independent of provider networking. Write scope: internal/llm and internal/tools tests.
acceptance_criteria: Harness verifies schema generation, successful Run, normal tool errors as fantasy text errors, and context cancellation behavior.

title: Test Client concurrent Set* and StreamChat race
description: Mandatory from MIGRATION_PLAN step 9. TestClientConcurrentSetAndStream must verify that SetBaseURL/SetModel/etc called from UI goroutine while StreamChat runs on agent goroutine does not race. Uses sync.WaitGroup with concurrent setters and a fake streamer. The mutex in Client must protect all cache access.

title: Test debug RoundTripper capture, stream-skip, and header redaction
description: internal/llm/debug.go has 0% coverage (~130 LoC untested). Tests must cover: (1) captures request body without consuming it, (2) captures JSON/plaintext response bodies, (3) SKIPS text/event-stream bodies (marks as '(stream)'), (4) redacts Authorization/Proxy-Authorization/X-Api-Key headers, (5) handles nil response and transport errors gracefully.

title: Test bitchteaTool Run never returns Go error + translateTools + splitSchema
description: internal/llm/tools.go has 0% coverage. Returning a Go error from bitchteaTool.Run aborts the entire fantasy stream. Tests must verify: (1) tool execution errors return NewTextErrorResponse not Go errors, (2) translateTools correctly maps Registry.Definitions to fantasy.AgentTool, (3) splitSchema handles []string, []any, and nil required inputs.

title: Test buildProvider host routing for all built-in profiles
description: Current provider-routing regression bead. Keep this as the Phase 9 prerequisite that locks existing host-routing behavior before replacing it with explicit Service identity. internal/llm/providers.go has low coverage. TestBuildProvider_RoutesByHost should cover every built-in profile in config.go: ollama to openaicompat, openrouter to openrouter, vercel to vercel, zai-openai to openaicompat, zai-anthropic to anthropic, and other OpenAI-compatible profiles to openaicompat. Include stripV1Suffix edge cases and hostOfMust panic on bad URL.
acceptance_criteria: Provider routing tests pass before Service identity refactor and give Phase 9 a stable baseline.

title: Phase 5: /models fuzzy finder for active profile
description: Add a /models command that lists models for the active profile/provider and opens a fuzzy-find picker. The immediate user workflow is /profile openrouter followed by /models, showing OpenRouter models without making the user paste model IDs manually. Should use the Phase 5 catalog/cache when available and provide an offline/fallback message when model listing cannot refresh.
acceptance_criteria: After /profile openrouter, /models opens a searchable model list; selecting a model updates the active model via existing cache-invalidating setters; errors explain whether catalog data is unavailable, offline, or provider unsupported; tests cover command routing and selection/fallback behavior.

title: Phase 6 epic: MCP integration
description: Integrate MCP via modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk and fantasy provider-defined tools without breaking local tools.
acceptance_criteria: MCP servers can be configured, tools/resources are discoverable, security boundaries are documented, and local tools still work.

title: Phase 9: move provider-specific feature gates to Service
description: Gate OpenRouter reasoning, Ollama usage suppression, Anthropic cache markers, and Z.ai quirks on Service instead of baseURL.
acceptance_criteria: Feature-gate tests use service identity and do not depend on hostname string matching.

title: Phase 9: expose service identity in profile commands and docs
description: Update profile save/load/display, docs, and command output to include or explain Service without confusing users.
acceptance_criteria: Profile commands preserve Service, mask keys, and docs explain Provider vs Service.

title: Phase 8: plumb cancellable context per tool call
description: Create per-tool contexts so one active tool can be cancelled without cancelling the full fantasy stream.
acceptance_criteria: Cancelling one tool stops its subprocess/read path when possible and leaves the turn context alive.

title: Phase 8: implement Esc key ladder in UI
description: Wire Esc x1/x2/x3 to panel close, tool cancel, turn cancel, and queue clear according to design.
acceptance_criteria: UI tests cover key sequences and state transitions without timing flakes.

title: Phase 8 epic: per-tool Esc cancellation
description: Implement Esc x1 tool cancellation that returns a user-cancelled tool result and lets fantasy continue the turn.
acceptance_criteria: Esc behavior matches REBUILD_NOTES, does not lose transcript state, and race tests pass.

title: Phase 7: daemon integration and failure-mode tests
description: Test daemon startup, shutdown, stale lock recovery, and TUI behavior without daemon.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes and at least one end-to-end daemon smoke is documented.

title: Phase 7: audit attic daemon code and current runtime needs
description: Read _attic daemon code and current session/memory/runtime requirements. Produce a keep/delete/rewrite map.
acceptance_criteria: Audit notes identify reusable pieces, deleted assumptions, and exact new package boundaries.

title: Phase 7: design daemon process and IPC model
description: Define foreground/TUI to daemon communication, lifecycle, locking, and crash recovery.
acceptance_criteria: Design covers single-instance behavior, logs, data paths, and how TUI remains usable when daemon is absent.

title: Phase 6: MCP integration tests and docs
description: Add fake MCP server tests, config examples, and user docs.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./..., fake MCP tool-call smoke, and docs for setup/troubleshooting are complete.

title: Phase 6: expose MCP tools through fantasy
description: Map MCP tool definitions to fantasy tools or provider-defined tools while avoiding name collisions with local tools.
acceptance_criteria: MCP tool schema conversion is tested; local tool names win or conflict deterministically per contract.

title: Phase 6: implement MCP client manager
description: Add lifecycle management for configured MCP servers. Write scope: new internal/mcp package and config tests.
acceptance_criteria: Client manager starts/stops servers cleanly, times out bad servers, and has fake-server tests.

title: Phase 5: model catalog/picker verification
description: Add regression tests and docs for catalog refresh, picker commands, and cost fallback.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./internal/config ./internal/llm ./internal/ui passes; docs explain cache and offline behavior.

title: Phase 5: implement model catalog autoupdate cache
description: Add a bounded HTTP/catalog refresh path for model metadata with offline fallback and no startup hangs.
acceptance_criteria: Refresh is timeout-bound, cache-backed, testable offline, and never blocks TUI startup indefinitely.

title: Phase 5: add model picker UI and commands
description: Expose model selection from cached catalog in TUI commands/profile flows. Write scope: internal/ui and config integration.
acceptance_criteria: Users can list/filter/select models; selections update Agent via cache-invalidating setters; UI tests cover command paths.

title: Phase 5: audit catwalk model/catalog APIs and cache shape
description: Inspect catwalk APIs in go.mod and decide cache file shape, TTL, and offline fallback behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Notes identify exact catwalk APIs, cache location, TTL, and failure behavior before UI work starts.

title: Phase 4: drain queued prompts through PrepareStep
description: Route queued autonomous or pending user prompts through a tested PrepareStep drain instead of ad hoc loop state.
acceptance_criteria: Queued prompt order is deterministic; cancellation prevents extra queued work; agent follow-up tests pass.

title: Phase 4: add Anthropic prompt-cache marker support
description: Use PrepareStep or provider options to add Anthropic cache_control markers where safe.
acceptance_criteria: Cache markers are provider-gated, tested with captured request payloads, and absent for non-Anthropic providers.

title: Phase 4 epic: real fantasy PrepareStep
description: Implement deferred PrepareStep responsibilities: cache hints, queued prompts, and dynamic tool refresh.
acceptance_criteria: PrepareStep behavior is tested, cancellable, and documented without regressing current streaming semantics.

title: Phase 3: move agent history to fantasy-native messages
description: Update Agent message history, compaction, restore, and follow-up flows to use the new canonical message representation.
acceptance_criteria: Agent tests cover bootstrap, restore, compaction, follow-up sanitization, and tool transcript preservation.

title: Phase 3: harden UI resume/fork/replay for new session format
description: Update UI/session integration tests and any transcript display assumptions after the message migration.
acceptance_criteria: Resume latest, fork, tool panel replay, and transcript rendering tests pass for both old and new session fixtures.

title: Phase 3: introduce fantasy-native message model behind adapters
description: Add internal adapters/types so agent and session can begin using fantasy.Message without breaking callers. Write scope: internal/llm convert/types tests.
acceptance_criteria: Adapters round-trip text, assistant tool calls, tool results, system prompts, and assistant-tail transcripts.

title: Phase 3 epic: fantasy-native messages end-to-end
description: Replace the JSONL-stable llm.Message boundary with fantasy message parts end-to-end while preserving session compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: Sessions resume, fork, save, and replay across old and new formats; no transcript reordering; full tests pass.

title: Phase 2: port bash and search_memory tools to typed wrappers
description: Implement typed params and fantasy.NewAgentTool wrappers for bash and search_memory. Keep subprocess and memory-scope behavior intact.
acceptance_criteria: Bash nonzero exits still return text; search_memory still respects scope; both have schema and execution tests.

title: Phase 2: replace generic Registry.Execute registration path
description: Switch llm tool registration from generic bitchteaTool plus args JSON dispatch to typed fantasy tools. Write scope: internal/llm/tools.go and adapter wiring.
acceptance_criteria: No Registry.Execute dependency from llm tool registration remains except compatibility code; full agent tool-loop tests pass.

title: Phase 2: port edit tool to typed wrapper
description: Implement typed params and fantasy.NewAgentTool wrapper for edit. Keep exact-match and non-unique failure behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Edit preserves current exact replacement semantics; malformed edit payloads become model-visible tool errors, not stream aborts.

title: Phase 2: port read and write tools to typed wrappers
description: Implement typed params and fantasy.NewAgentTool wrappers for read and write. Write scope: new typed wrapper files plus tests for read/write only.
acceptance_criteria: Read/write preserve current behavior, schema is valid through fantasy, and tests cover success plus parse/file errors.

title: Phase 2 epic: typed fantasy tools
description: Rewrite local tools as typed fantasy.NewAgentTool wrappers so llm no longer hand-builds JSON-string tool calls through Registry.Execute.
acceptance_criteria: All tool behavior preserved; no malformed provider schemas; existing tool smoke and full Go tests pass.

title: Implement graduated Esc cancellation per REBUILD_NOTES.md
description: No Esc key handler exists in model.go. REBUILD_NOTES.md specifies 3-stage graduation: Esc x1 = cancel tool-in-flight (turn continues), Esc x2 = full turn cancel, Esc x3 = turn cancel + clear queue. Must occur within ~1.5s window. Architecturally tricky because fantasy owns the dispatch timing — need per-tool-call context.WithCancel and a way to interrupt Run mid-execution.

title: Test fantasyToLLM edge cases for coverage gap
description: convert.go:fantasyToLLM is only 48% covered. Missing branches: pointer parts (*TextPart, *ToolCallPart, *ToolResultPart), multi-tool-call assistant messages, mixed content+tool-call parts. Fix coverage to 90%+.

title: Test safeSend context cancellation and toolResultText edge cases
description: internal/llm/stream.go has 0% coverage. Tests must cover: (1) safeSend blocks on normal send, returns ctx.Err() on cancelled context, (2) toolResultText handles TextPart, pointer TextPart, nil pointer, and unknown output types returning empty string.

title: Phase 7: add daemon CLI/config integration
description: Expose daemon start/status/stop or equivalent configuration in CLI without disrupting TUI startup.
acceptance_criteria: CLI commands have tests and sensible errors when daemon is unavailable.

title: Phase 7: implement daemon session jobs
description: Implement janitor, heartbeat, or session-maintenance jobs selected by the design.
acceptance_criteria: Jobs are idempotent, bounded, and tested against temp session dirs.

title: Phase 7 epic: daemon rebuild
description: Rebuild the attic daemon into the current architecture for background session/runtime tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon has a clear process model, safe state ownership, tests, and no stale attic dependencies.

title: Phase 6: support MCP resources and prompts discovery
description: Expose MCP resources/prompts in a bounded way for agent context or user commands.
acceptance_criteria: Resource listing and selective read paths are tested; large resources are bounded and cancellable.

title: Phase 5: sync CostTracker pricing with catalog data
description: Wire cached catwalk pricing into CostTracker while preserving static fallback behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Known model costs still compute offline; refreshed catalog prices are used when available; cost tests cover both paths.

title: Phase 5 epic: catwalk HTTP autoupdate and model picker
description: Use catwalk model/catalog data for pricing refresh and interactive model selection.
acceptance_criteria: Model metadata updates safely, model picker is usable, and cost tracking stays accurate across configured providers.

title: Phase 4: support per-step dynamic tool refresh hook
description: Add a hook for refreshing available tools before each step so future MCP tools can appear without rebuilding the agent loop.
acceptance_criteria: Tool refresh has a fake-registry test and does not mutate shared registry state unsafely.

title: Phase 3: remove legacy llm.Message conversion shims
description: Delete obsolete conversion code once all production paths are fantasy-native. Keep only explicit legacy-session migration code.
acceptance_criteria: No production call path depends on splitForFantasy/fantasyToLLM compatibility shims; docs and tests reflect final boundary.

title: IRC-style channel/query routing runtime
description: Open TODO.md item (bitchtea-nyd). Implement routing commands /join /msg /part /query. Make Enter send to current focus. Keep slash commands working regardless of focus. This is the foundation for the IRC-style architecture.
